Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal: The objective is to analyze a provided event log of a Customer Support Ticket Resolution process. I need to identify cases with long resolution times, determine the root causes bottlenecks, delays, escalations, and provide recommendations to improve performance.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Data: Case 101: Start: 2024-03-01 08:00 End: 2024-03-01 10:15 Total Duration: 2 hours 15 minutes. Flow: Receive -> Triage 10m -> Assign 10m -> Investigate 40m -> Resolve 60m -> Close 15m. Observation: Very fast, no escalation. Case 103: Total Duration: 1 hour 20 minutes. Observation: Very fast, no escalation.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 102: Total Duration: ~25 hours 10 minutes. Flow: 09:00 Assign to L1 11:30 Escalate to L2 Gap from Assign: 2.5 hrs 14:00 Investigate Issue Gap from Escalate: 2.5 hrs 09:00 Resolve Next Day Gap from Investigate: ~19 hours. Observation: Significant delay between Investigation L2 and Resolution. Escalation occurred.

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait, check timestamps for Case 104: 09:30 Assign 13:00 Investigate Gap: 3.5 hours 08:00 Resolve Next Day Gap: ~19 hours. For Case 105: 09:10 Investigate L1 10:00 Escalate 03-02 14:00 Investigate Gap: ~28 hours 03-03 09:00 Resolve Gap: ~19 hours.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Synthesize Findings Task 1: Identify Slow Cases. Fast: 101 2h 15m, 103 1h 20m. Slow: 102 ~25h, 104 ~24h, 105 ~49h. Outlier: 105 is the worst.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Task 2: Root Causes. Escalations: Cases 102 and 105 have escalations. Case 104 does not, but is still slow. Waiting Times Inter-activity gaps: 102: 19-hour gap between L2 Investigation and Resolution. 104: 19-hour gap between Investigation and Resolution. 105: 28-hour gap between Escalation and L2 Investigation. 19-hour gap between L2 Investigation and Resolution. Overnight waits suggest work during business hours, tickets sit overnight.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Self-Correction Double Check: Did I calculate Case 105 correctly? Start: Mar 1 08:25 End: Mar 3 09:30 Total: ~49 hours. Correct. Case 104: ~24 hours 10 mins. Correct. Case 102: ~25 hours 10 mins. Correct. Is Case 104s delay due to escalation? No, there is no Escalate row for 104. Conclusion: The overnight gap is the biggest consistent factor.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Final Polish: Pattern: The Overnight Gap approx 19-28 hours is the primary driver. Secondary Pattern: Escalations add complexity and handover time. Tertiary Pattern: Triage Assignment efficiency varies Case 104 vs 101 103.
